metadata.title: DateField
metadata.description: Allow users to select a specific date with a date picker.

properties.description: Dates that can be selected. A comma-separated list of dates, date ranges. Whitespace is allowed after commas. The default '' allows all dates. Dates in YYYY-MM-DD format allow a single date.Dates in YYYY-MM format allow a whole month.Dates in YYYY format allow a whole year.Ranges are expressed as start--end. - Ranges are inclusive.If either start or end is omitted, the range is unbounded in that direction.If parts of the date are omitted for start, they are assumed to be the minimum possible value. So 2024-- is equivalent to 2024-01-01--.If parts of the date are omitted for end, they are assumed to be the maximum possible value. So --2024 is equivalent to --2024-12-31.Whitespace is allowed either side of --.

Days of the week that can be selected. These intersect with the result of allow and disallow. A comma-separated list of days. Whitespace is allowed after commas. The default '' has no effect on the result of allow and disallow. Days are sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday.

A hint as to the intended content of the field. When set to on (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents. When set to off, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes. Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.
The default value for the field.
Default month to display in YYYY-MM format. This value is used until view is set, either directly or as a result of user interaction. Defaults to the current month in the user's locale.

Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user. This will also be exposed to screen reader users.
Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

Dates that cannot be selected. These subtract from allow. A comma-separated list of dates, date ranges. Whitespace is allowed after commas. The default '' has no effect on allow. Dates in YYYY-MM-DD format disallow a single date.Dates in YYYY-MM format disallow a whole month.Dates in YYYY format disallow a whole year.Ranges are expressed as start--end. - Ranges are inclusive.If either start or end is omitted, the range is unbounded in that direction.If parts of the date are omitted for start, they are assumed to be the minimum possible value. So 2024-- is equivalent to 2024-01-01--.If parts of the date are omitted for end, they are assumed to be the maximum possible value. So --2024 is equivalent to --2024-12-31.Whitespace is allowed either side of --.

Days of the week that cannot be selected. This subtracts from allowDays, and intersects with the result of allow and disallow. A comma-separated list of days. Whitespace is allowed after commas. The default '' has no effect on allowDays. Days are sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday.
Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.
A unique identifier for the element.
Content to use as the field label.
Changes the visibility of the component's label. visible: the label is visible to all users.exclusive: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.
An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.
A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.
The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the error property.
The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.
Displayed month in YYYY-MM format. onViewChange is called when this value changes. Defaults to defaultView.

examples.name: Basic usage
examples.description: Simple date field for collecting a single date with a descriptive label.

examples.name: With default value
examples.description: Date field pre-populated with a specific date for editing existing data.

examples.name: With date restrictions
examples.description: Shows how to restrict selectable dates to weekdays only.
examples.html_code:
<s-date-field
  label="Delivery date"
  name="deliveryDate"
  disallowDays="[0, 6]"
  details="Delivery available Monday through Friday only"
></s-date-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.date_field :delivery_date, label: "Delivery date", disallowdays: "[0, 6]", details: "Delivery available Monday through Friday only" %>

examples.name: With specific allowed dates
examples.description: Demonstrates allowing only specific dates from a predefined list.
examples.html_code:
<s-date-field
  label="Available appointment dates"
  name="appointmentDate"
  allow='["2024-03-20", "2024-03-22", "2024-03-25", "2024-03-27"]'
  details="Select from available time slots"
></s-date-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.date_field :appointment_date, label: "Available appointment dates", allow: "[\"2024-03-20\", \"2024-03-22\", \"2024-03-25\", \"2024-03-27\"]", details: "Select from available time slots" %>

examples.name: With error state
examples.description: Date field showing validation error for required field or invalid date entry.
examples.html_code:
<s-date-field
  label="Event date"
  name="eventDate"
  required
  error="Select a valid event date"
></s-date-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.date_field :event_date, label: "Event date", required: true, error: "Select a valid event date" %>

examples.name: Disabled and read-only states
examples.description: Shows date fields in different interaction states for viewing-only or disabled forms.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-date-field
    label="Creation date"
    name="createdDate"
    value="2024-01-01"
    readOnly
  ></s-date-field>

  <s-date-field
    label="Archived date"
    name="archivedDate"
    value="2024-02-15"
    disabled
  ></s-date-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.date_field :created_date, label: "Creation date", value: "2024-01-01", readonly: true %>

  <%= form.date_field :archived_date, label: "Archived date", value: "2024-02-15", disabled: true %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Form integration
examples.description: Complete form example showing date field with other form elements.
examples.html_code:
<form>
  <s-stack gap="base">
    <s-text-field
      label="Customer name"
      name="customerName"
      required
    ></s-text-field>

    <s-date-field
      label="Order date"
      name="orderDate"
      value="2024-03-15"
      required
    ></s-date-field>

    <s-date-field
      label="Requested delivery date"
      name="deliveryDate"
      disallowDays="[0, 6]"
      details="Weekdays only"
    ></s-date-field>

    <s-button type="submit" variant="primary">Process order</s-button>
  </s-stack>
</form>
examples.erb_code:
<form>
  <s-stack gap="base">
    <%= form.text_field :customer_name, label: "Customer name", required: true %>

    <%= form.date_field :order_date, label: "Order date", value: "2024-03-15", required: true %>

    <%= form.date_field :delivery_date, label: "Requested delivery date", disallowdays: "[0, 6]", details: "Weekdays only" %>

    <s-button type="submit" variant="primary">Process order</s-button>
  </s-stack>
</form>

examples.name: Date range selection
examples.description: Example showing two date fields for selecting a date range.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-heading>Sales report period</s-heading>

  <s-stack direction="inline" gap="base">
    <s-date-field
      label="Start date"
      name="startDate"
      id="report-start"
    ></s-date-field>

    <s-date-field
      label="End date"
      name="endDate"
      id="report-end"
    ></s-date-field>
  </s-stack>

  <s-button variant="primary">Generate report</s-button>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-heading>Sales report period</s-heading>

  <s-stack direction="inline" gap="base">
    <%= form.date_field :start_date, label: "Start date", id: "report-start" %>

    <%= form.date_field :end_date, label: "End date", id: "report-end" %>
  </s-stack>

  <s-button variant="primary">Generate report</s-button>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Date fields with validation
examples.description: Demonstrates date fields with business logic restrictions and validation.
examples.html_code:
<s-section>
  <s-heading>Promotion settings</s-heading>
  <s-stack gap="base">
    <s-text-field
      label="Promotion name"
      name="promoName"
      value="Spring sale"
    ></s-text-field>

    <s-date-field
      label="Start date"
      name="promoStart"
      value="2024-04-01"
      details="Promotion begins at midnight"
    ></s-date-field>

    <s-date-field
      label="End date"
      name="promoEnd"
      value="2024-04-30"
      details="Promotion ends at 11:59 PM"
    ></s-date-field>

    <s-checkbox
      name="autoPublish"
      label="Automatically publish on start date"
    ></s-checkbox>
  </s-stack>
</s-section>
examples.erb_code:
<s-section>
  <s-heading>Promotion settings</s-heading>
  <s-stack gap="base">
    <%= form.text_field :promo_name, label: "Promotion name", value: "Spring sale" %>

    <%= form.date_field :promo_start, label: "Start date", value: "2024-04-01", details: "Promotion begins at midnight" %>

    <%= form.date_field :promo_end, label: "End date", value: "2024-04-30", details: "Promotion ends at 11:59 PM" %>

    <%= form.check_box :auto_publish, label: "Automatically publish on start date" %>
  </s-stack>
</s-section>

examples.name: Date field validation
examples.description: Interactive example showing required date field validation with dynamic error messages.
examples.html_code:
<s-date-field
  label="Event date"
  name="eventDate"
  required
  error="Select a valid event date"
></s-date-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.date_field :event_date, label: "Event date", required: true, error: "Select a valid event date" %>
